verdict: High AI displacement risk due to routine_cognitive and ai_capability_gap scores, but mitigated by regulatory_moat and physical_dexterity dimensions.

reasoning_steps.title: Routine cognition is highly automated
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks like monitoring engine operation, fuel consumption, and functioning of aircraft systems during flights are pattern-matching on text/data that current frontier LLMs can handle.

reasoning_steps.title: AI capability gap is high
reasoning_steps.body: The ability to start engines, operate controls, and pilot airplanes is already being performed by AI tools in various industries, making the job of a pilot more susceptible to automation.

adjacent_careers.name: Air Traffic Controller
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Both pilots and air traffic controllers rely on complex decision-making and situational awareness, making them adjacent careers.

adjacent_careers.name: Flight Dispatcher
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Flight dispatchers and pilots work together to ensure safe and efficient flight operations, making them related but distinct careers.

recent_developments.headline: Anthropic Economic Index maps real AI usage across the workforce
recent_developments.impact: Use is concentrated in writing, coding, and analysis — physical and interpersonal work see far less AI involvement.

recent_developments.headline: OECD: about 60% of jobs in advanced economies are exposed to AI
recent_developments.impact: Exposure does not mean replacement — most exposed roles are expected to be transformed rather than eliminated.

recent_developments.headline: Labor market shifts toward AI-complementary skills
recent_developments.impact: Postings increasingly ask for judgment, verification, and direction of AI tools — not just task execution.
